programs.name: Nevada's Senior Rx Program

eligibility.income_limits: As of July 2022: $33,152 annually for singles; $44,193 for married couples. Income limits adjusted annually on July 1st based on Consumer Price Index. (Note: Earlier 2006 limits were $23,175 singles, $30,168 couples)[2][3]
eligibility.asset_limits: As of December 2022: $15,510 for singles; $30,950 for married couples. No asset test was associated with the program in earlier years[2][3]
other_requirements: Continuous Nevada residency for at least 12 months prior to application date[1][2]
Applicants eligible for Medicare Part D were required to enroll in Medicare Prescription Drug Plan first and exhaust Extra Help benefits before using Senior Rx[3]

benefits.value: Prescription medication cost assistance; specific dollar amounts or coverage limits not detailed in available documentation
benefits.varies_by: Medicare Part D eligibility status (wrap-around coverage for Medicare-eligible vs. direct assistance for non-Medicare-eligible)

methods: Mail: Nevada Senior Rx, 4126 Technology Way, Suite 101, Carson City, NV 89706-2009[1]
Online application download available through program website (specific URL not provided in sources)
forms: Application form available for download (specific form number not provided)[1]
documents: Proof of income (current tax return and/or credible documentation of income source)[3]
Proof of Nevada residency for 12 consecutive months

geography.restrictions: Nevada residency required; no county-specific restrictions mentioned
geography.regional_variations: Phone line variation: 687-7555 for Reno-Carson City-Gardnerville areas; 1-(866) 303-6323 for other areas[1]
offices_or_providers: Administered through Nevada Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS) Director's Office[1]
Main office: 4126 Technology Way, Suite 101, Carson City, NV 89706-2009

current_alternatives.extra_help: Medicare Extra Help program for Part D costs (income limit as of December 2022: $20,628 singles, $27,708 couples; resource limit: $15,510 singles, $30,950 couples)[3]
current_alternatives.medicaid: Medicaid may cover prescription costs for eligible individuals[6]
current_alternatives.nevada_map: Nevada Medicare Assistance Program (MAP) for Medicare Part D plan assistance: 800-307-4444, NevadaMAP@adsd.nv.gov, www.nevadacareconnection.org[6]

gotchas: Program is no longer active as of December 31, 2023[6]
Medicare-eligible applicants were required to use Medicare Part D and Extra Help first; Senior Rx was secondary coverage only[3]
Income limits changed annually on July 1st, making historical comparisons difficult[2]
Eligibility was redetermined annually with required notification letters[4]
As of May 2006, only 7.6% of Senior Rx members were non-Medicare-eligible, indicating the program primarily served Medicare beneficiaries[2]
programs.data_shape_notes: Program operated as wrap-around coverage for Medicare Part D beneficiaries while also serving non-Medicare-eligible seniors. Income and asset limits adjusted annually. Program was administered at state level through DHHS with regional phone support. Now defunct; families should contact Nevada Medicare Assistance Program for current prescription assistance options.
programs._raw:
I need to provide important context before delivering the JSON response: **The Nevada Senior Rx Program ended on December 31, 2023**[6], so this program is no longer accepting applications or enrolling new members.

However, here's what families should know about this now-defunct program, along with current alternatives:

```json
{
  "name": "Nevada's Senior Rx Program",
  "status": "PROGRAM ENDED December 31, 2023",
  "eligibility": {
    "age": 62,
    "income_limits": "As of July 2022: $33,152 annually for singles; $44,193 for married couples. Income limits adjusted annually on July 1st based on Consumer Price Index. (Note: Earlier 2006 limits were $23,175 singles, $30,168 couples)[2][3]",
    "asset_limits": "As of December 2022: $15,510 for singles; $30,950 for married couples. No asset test was associated with the program in earlier years[2][3]",
    "other_requirements": [
      "Continuous Nevada residency for at least 12 months prior to application date[1][2]",
      "Applicants eligible for Medicare Part D were required to enroll in Medicare Prescription Drug Plan first and exhaust Extra Help benefits before using Senior Rx[3]"
    ]
  },
  "benefits": {
    "type": "financial",
    "value": "Prescription medication cost assistance; specific dollar amounts or coverage limits not detailed in available documentation",
    "varies_by": "Medicare Part D eligibility status (wrap-around coverage for Medicare-eligible vs. direct assistance for non-Medicare-eligible)"
  },
  "application": {
    "methods": [
      "Mail: Nevada Senior Rx, 4126 Technology Way, Suite 101, Carson City, NV 89706-2009[1]",
      "Phone: 1-(866) 303-6323 or 687-7555 (for Reno-Carson City-Gardnerville areas)[1]",
      "Online application download available through program website (specific URL not provided in sources)"
    ],
    "processing_time": "Not specified in available documentation",
    "waitlist": "Not mentioned in available documentation",
    "forms": [
      "Application form available for download (specific form number not provided)[1]"
    ],
    "documents": [
      "Proof of income (current tax return and/or credible documentation of income source)[3]",
      "Proof of Nevada residency for 12 consecutive months"
    ]
  },
  "geography": {
    "statewide": true,
    "restrictions": "Nevada residency required; no county-specific restrictions mentioned",
    "regional_variations": "Phone line variation: 687-7555 for Reno-Carson City-Gardnerville areas; 1-(866) 303-6323 for other areas[1]",
    "offices_or_providers": [
      "Administered through Nevada Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS) Director's Office[1]",
      "Main office: 4126 Technology Way, Suite 101, Carson City, NV 89706-2009"
    ]
  },
  "official_source": "https://adsd.nv.gov/programs/seniors/seniorrx/srrxprog/ (now shows program closure notice)[6]",
  "current_alternatives": {
    "extra_help": "Medicare Extra Help program for Part D costs (income limit as of December 2022: $20,628 singles, $27,708 couples; resource limit: $15,510 singles, $30,950 couples)[3]",
    "medicaid": "Medicaid may cover prescription costs for eligible individuals[6]",
    "nevada_map": "Nevada Medicare Assistance Program (MAP) for Medicare Part D plan assistance: 800-307-4444, NevadaMAP@adsd.nv.gov, www.nevadacareconnection.org[6]"
  },
  "gotchas": [
    "Program is no longer active as of December 31, 2023[6]",
    "Medicare-eligible applicants were required to use Medicare Part D and Extra Help first; Senior Rx was secondary coverage only[3]",
    "Income limits changed annually on July 1st, making historical comparisons difficult[2]",
    "Eligibility was redetermined annually with required notification letters[4]",
    "As of May 2006, only 7.6% of Senior Rx members were non-Medicare-eligible, indicating the program primarily served Medicare beneficiaries[2]"
  ],
  "data_shape_notes": "Program operated as wrap-around coverage for Medicare Part D beneficiaries while also serving non-Medicare-eligible seniors. Income and asset limits adjusted annually. Program was administered at state level through DHHS with regional phone support. Now defunct; families should contact Nevada Medicare Assistance Program for current prescription assistance options."
}
```

**For families seeking help now:** Contact the Nevada Medicare Assistance Program at 800-307-4444 to explore Extra Help, Medicaid, or other current prescription assistance options[6].

programs.name: Home and Community-Based Waiver for the Frail Elderly (HCBW-FE)

eligibility.income_limits: 2025 limits: Individual applicant: $2,901/month. Married spouses (both applying): $5,802/month. When only one spouse applies, individual income limit applies and spouse’s income is disregarded[3].
eligibility.asset_limits: 2025 limits: Individual: $2,000. Married spouses (both applying): $3,000. If only one spouse applies, both spouses' assets are considered jointly owned and limited to individual threshold. Exempt assets include primary home (if applicant lives there or intends to return with equity ≤$752,000 in 2026; spouse, dependent child under 21, or blind/disabled child living there), household furnishings, and one vehicle[1][2][3].
other_requirements: Require Nursing Facility Level of Care (NFLOC): Minimum score of 3 functional deficits on Nevada Medicaid LOC assessment (covers self-admin meds/treatments, ADLs like bathing/dressing/continence/transferring/mobility/eating, IADLs like meals/homemaking, supervision needs). At risk of nursing home placement within 30 days without waiver services[1][2][4]
Living in home, loved one's home, group residential facility, or assisted living (varies by sub-category)[3][4]
Medical costs for home care less than institutionalization (determined by ADSD)[4]
Meet institutional Medicaid financial/non-financial criteria except institutional residence[4]

benefits.value: Case management, homemaker, respite, adult companion, adult day care, augmented personal care, chore, home delivered meals, personal emergency response system. Must require at least one monthly service. No fixed dollar amounts or hours specified; services promote community living to avoid nursing home[5][6].

methods: Contact Office of Community Living (OCL) for Intake Home and Community Based application[1]
In-person or over-the-phone interview for functional NFLOC assessment[3]
Determined in combination with agency administering waiver (ADSD/DHCFP)[4]
application.waitlist: Not mentioned; may exist due to waiver caps (common for HCBS)[null]
forms: Office of Community Living (OCL) Intake Home and Community Based application[1]
documents: Financial records (income/assets proof), medical/functional assessment via interview, proof of residency, home equity details if applicable[1][3]

geography.restrictions: None; available statewide for eligible Nevada residents[3]
geography.regional_variations: Administered via ADSD/DHCFP district offices; potential differences in providers/offices/wait times by region (e.g., Las Vegas-specific info noted), but core rules uniform[4][7]
offices_or_providers: Office of Community Living (OCL), Aging and Disability Services Division (ADSD), Division of Health Care Finance and Policy (DHCFP)[1][4]

programs.official_source: https://dwss.nv.gov/ (DWSS) or https://adsd.nv.gov/ (ADSD); waiver details at https://www.medicaid.gov/medicaid/section-1115-demo/demonstration-and-waiver-list/Waiver-Descript-Factsheet/NV[4][6]
gotchas: Home exempt for eligibility but subject to Medicaid estate recovery without planning[2]
Dementia diagnosis does not automatically qualify; must meet NFLOC score of 3+ deficits[1][2]
Spousal assets jointly counted even if only one applies[3]
Must show home care costs less than nursing home[4]
2026 home equity limit $752,000; confirm current figure as limits adjust[1][2]
programs.data_shape_notes: Financial limits provided for 2025 (income by marital status, assets joint for couples); NFLOC via scored functional assessment (min 3 deficits); services fixed list, statewide but agency-coordinated; sub-categories for living settings (home/group/assisted living)

programs.name: Community Options Program for the Elderly (COPE)

eligibility.income_limits: Financially eligible according to monthly income and asset guidelines established by NAC 427A; call ADSD for current income guidelines (2025 OCL Monthly Income Limits available on site). Exact dollar amounts and household size variations not specified in sources—must contact regional office.[1][2]
eligibility.asset_limits: Subject to asset guidelines per NAC 427A; specific limits, what counts, and exemptions not detailed—verification required by ADSD including financial documentation.[2]
other_requirements: At risk of institutionalization (nursing home placement) if services not provided; meet Nursing Facility Level of Care (NFLOC), often within 30 days without services.[1][3][6]
Reside in Nevada at home (services only while residing at home).[2]
U.S. Citizen or alien legally admitted for permanent residency.[2]

benefits.value: Case Management, Homemaker, Social Adult Day Care, Adult Companion, Attendant Care (Personal Care), Personal Emergency Response System (PERS), Chore, Respite. May allow certain family members (excluding spouses and legal guardians) to be paid for personal care services. Similar to HCBS FE Waiver but non-Medicaid.[1][3][5][6]

methods: Phone: Call local regional ADSD office to provide information over phone to intake team member.
In-person or mail: Fill out OCL Program Application and submit to nearest ADSD office. List of regional offices on adsd.nv.gov.
Online: Download forms from adsd.nv.gov (no fully online submission specified).
application.processing_time: Intake case manager contacts after completed/signed application received; screening/assessment follows to determine eligibility and services. No specific timeline stated.[1]
application.waitlist: Possible waitlist; screening completed if placed on waitlist, full assessment if not. Used as bridge while waiting for HCBS FE Waiver.[2][6]
forms: OCL Program Application (English and Spanish versions)
State Application for Assistance (for financial eligibility)
documents: Proof of U.S. citizenship or legal residency.
Medical info for NFLOC determination (screening at face-to-face interview).

geography.regional_variations: Administered through regional ADSD offices with potential differences in wait times, providers, or processing; contact nearest office. Examples: Las Vegas (2460 Professional Court, Suite 110), Henderson (2831 St. Rose Parkway, Suite 200).[1][7]
offices_or_providers: Regional ADSD offices statewide (full list on adsd.nv.gov)

gotchas: Non-Medicaid program serving as bridge for HCBS FE Waiver waitlist—may transition if Medicaid-eligible.[1][6]
Must meet strict NFLOC (risk of nursing home within 30 days); not for general aging support.[3]
Income/asset limits change (e.g., 2025 limits referenced)—always call for current figures.[1]
Services only while residing at home in Nevada; family paid caregivers excluded if spouses/guardians.[5]
Priority given (varies by tier, not detailed).[3]
programs.data_shape_notes: Statewide with regional offices; financial eligibility via changing guidelines (no fixed table in sources); waitlist common as non-Medicaid alternative to HCBS FE Waiver; NFLOC required with imminent risk focus.

programs.name: Social Security Domiciliary Rate

eligibility.income_limits: Must receive SSI with income below the Domiciliary Rate level (specific dollar amount not detailed in sources; SSI maximum in 2026 is $994 individual/$1,491 couple, but Domiciliary Rate supplements if SSI is lower). General SSI limits: countable income under federal benefit rate, assets under $2,000 individual/$3,000 couple. Varies by living arrangement (e.g., OSS C for domiciliary care facilities).[2][3][5][6]
eligibility.asset_limits: SSI standard: $2,000 individual, $3,000 couple. Exempt: home (if valued under $595,000), usually one car, burial plot, certain other resources.[1][3]
other_requirements: Living in a licensed Residential Facility for Groups (RFG) or Assisted Living Facility (ALF) providing personal care services.
At risk of nursing home placement without services.
Aged, blind, or disabled per SSI rules.

benefits.value: Increase in SSI payment to the 'Domiciliary Rate' (Nevada Optional State Supplementation OSS C level for residents in licensed private non-medical domiciliary care facilities; exact 2026 dollar amount not specified, administered by SSA as supplement to federal SSI).[2][6]

methods: In-person at local Social Security office: Bring RFG/ALF admission document, facility business license, federal EIN number.[2]
Phone: Senior Focus Team at 702-672-5611 for questions/application guidance; SSA at 800-772-1213 to schedule SSI appointment.[1][2]
Email: Senior Focus Team (details via phone).[2]
forms: SSI application (no specific form number listed; standard SSI process via SSA office).
documents: Proof of SSI eligibility, income, assets (standard SSI docs: ID, income statements, bank info, etc.).

geography.restrictions: Nevada residents only; facilities must be licensed/authorized by State of Nevada.
geography.regional_variations: Local Social Security offices handle in-person applications; Senior Focus Team phone appears Las Vegas-area (702 area code), but program statewide via SSA.[2][6]
offices_or_providers: Local Social Security offices statewide; contact Senior Focus Team at 702-672-5611.

gotchas: Not a standalone program—requires existing SSI eligibility and move to licensed RFG/ALF.[2][6]
Facility must be state-licensed private non-medical providing personal care to unrelated adults; public institutions serving >16 may not qualify.[6]
Income/assets assessed under strict SSI rules; home equity cap at $595,000.[1]
Must apply for other benefits; SSA administers but state rules apply.[3][6]
People miss that it's an SSI supplement increase, not separate aid.
programs.data_shape_notes: Tied to SSI with Nevada Optional State Supplementation (OSS C) for specific licensed domiciliary care facilities; no standalone income table, scales with SSI living arrangement; statewide but facility-licensing required.

programs.name: Taxi Assistance Program (TAP)

eligibility.income_limits: Monthly income below 125% of the Federal Poverty Guidelines (effective July 1, 2025) for applicant and/or spouse. Exact dollar amounts vary by household size and are based on current FPG; proof required from all sources. Older documents mention 300% FPG, but latest is 125%[1][2]. No full table provided in sources.
other_requirements: OR permanent disability (any age): Provide physician letter or Social Security Award letter[1][2][3]
Proof of income from all sources for applicant and/or spouse[1][2]

benefits.value: Discounted taxicab coupon booklets accepted by all taxicab companies in Clark County. Example: Books with 20 $1 coupons or 4 $5 coupons ($20 value) purchased for $10. Tier categories exist (1-5) but specifics not detailed; coupons must be redeemed by expiration date[2][5][6].

methods: Mail: Complete Taxi Assistance Program Registration Form to Aging and Disability Services Division - Las Vegas Regional Office, 7150 Pollock Drive, Las Vegas, NV 89119[1]

In-person by appointment only: 7150 Pollock Drive, Las Vegas, NV 89119 or 3320 W. Sahara Ave., Suite 100, Las Vegas, NV 89102. Cash, personal checks, or money orders only (no credit/debit cards)[1][3]
Phone for info/appointment assistance: (702) 486-3581, Monday-Friday 8:00 a.m.-4:30 p.m. (excl. holidays)[1]
application.processing_time: Not specified; applications reviewed and notices issued[1].
application.waitlist: Funding capacity met as of latest update; new applicants processed but no additional funding available, receive notice of decision[1].
documents: Proof of income: Previous year's Federal Tax Return or IRS Tax Transcript OR most recent bank statement (all pages, showing deposits) AND Current Social Security Award Letter or SNAP Award Letter[1][2]
For disability: Physician letter or Social Security Award letter[1][2][3]

geography.regional_variations: Clark County-specific; all taxicab companies in area participate. No info on other Nevada regions[1][4].
offices_or_providers: Aging and Disability Services Division - Las Vegas Regional Office, 7150 Pollock Drive, Las Vegas, NV 89119[1]
3320 W. Sahara Ave., Suite 100, Las Vegas, NV 89102[3]
1860 E Sahara Ave, Las Vegas, NV 89104[6]

gotchas: Currently at funding capacity; new applicants approved but no coupons issued until funding available[1]
Clark County residents only—not statewide[1][4]
Appointments required for in-person; no drop-ins. Cash/check/money order only[1]
Income at 125% FPG (recent) vs. older 300% references—use latest[1][2][3]
Must prove permanent disability with specific docs if under 60[2]
Coupons expire and rides may need advance scheduling[5][6]
programs.data_shape_notes: Clark County-restricted; tiered categories (1-5) for eligibility/benefits; income test at 125% FPG with proof from all sources including spouse; currently closed to new funded enrollments
programs._raw:
{
  "name": "Taxi Assistance Program (TAP)",
  "eligibility": {
    "age": 60,
    "income_limits": "Monthly income below 125% of the Federal Poverty Guidelines (effective July 1, 2025) for applicant and/or spouse. Exact dollar amounts vary by household size and are based on current FPG; proof required from all sources. Older documents mention 300% FPG, but latest is 125%[1][2]. No full table provided in sources.",
    "asset_limits": "No asset limits mentioned[1][2][3].",
    "other_requirements": [
      "Nevada resident (specifically Clark County resident)[1][2]",
      "OR permanent disability (any age): Provide physician letter or Social Security Award letter[1][2][3]",
      "Proof of income from all sources for applicant and/or spouse[1][2]"
    ]
  },
  "benefits": {
    "type": "in_kind",
    "value": "Discounted taxicab coupon booklets accepted by all taxicab companies in Clark County. Example: Books with 20 $1 coupons or 4 $5 coupons ($20 value) purchased for $10. Tier categories exist (1-5) but specifics not detailed; coupons must be redeemed by expiration date[2][5][6].",
    "varies_by": "priority_tier"
  },
  "application": {
    "methods": [
      "Mail: Complete Taxi Assistance Program Registration Form to Aging and Disability Services Division - Las Vegas Regional Office, 7150 Pollock Drive, Las Vegas, NV 89119[1]",
      "In-person by appointment only: 7150 Pollock Drive, Las Vegas, NV 89119 or 3320 W. Sahara Ave., Suite 100, Las Vegas, NV 89102. Cash, personal checks, or money orders only (no credit/debit cards)[1][3]",
      "Phone for info/appointment assistance: (702) 486-3581, Monday-Friday 8:00 a.m.-4:30 p.m. (excl. holidays)[1]"
    ],
    "processing_time": "Not specified; applications reviewed and notices issued[1].",
    "waitlist": "Funding capacity met as of latest update; new applicants processed but no additional funding available, receive notice of decision[1].",
    "forms": [
      "Taxi Assistance Program Registration Form (Revised 01/2024)[2]"
    ],
    "documents": [
      "Nevada Photo ID/Driver's License[1][2][3]",
      "Proof of income: Previous year's Federal Tax Return or IRS Tax Transcript OR most recent bank statement (all pages, showing deposits) AND Current Social Security Award Letter or SNAP Award Letter[1][2]",
      "For disability: Physician letter or Social Security Award letter[1][2][3]"
    ]
  },
  "geography": {
    "statewide": false,
    "restrictions": "Clark County, Nevada only[1][4][6]",
    "regional_variations": "Clark County-specific; all taxicab companies in area participate. No info on other Nevada regions[1][4].",
    "offices_or_providers": [
      "Aging and Disability Services Division - Las Vegas Regional Office, 7150 Pollock Drive, Las Vegas, NV 89119[1]",
      "3320 W. Sahara Ave., Suite 100, Las Vegas, NV 89102[3]",
      "1860 E Sahara Ave, Las Vegas, NV 89104[6]"
    ]
  },
  "official_source": "https://adsd.nv.gov/programs/seniors/tap/tap_prog/[1]",
  "gotchas": [
    "Currently at funding capacity; new applicants approved but no coupons issued until funding available[1]",
    "Clark County residents only—not statewide[1][4]",
    "Appointments required for in-person; no drop-ins. Cash/check/money order only[1]",
    "Income at 125% FPG (recent) vs. older 300% references—use latest[1][2][3]",
    "Must prove permanent disability with specific docs if under 60[2]",
    "Coupons expire and rides may need advance scheduling[5][6]"
  ],
  "data_shape_notes": "Clark County-restricted; tiered categories (1-5) for eligibility/benefits; income test at 125% FPG with proof from all sources including spouse; currently closed to new funded enrollments"
